endorphins.title: Endorphins

stats.born: Your pituitary gland and hypothalamus, deep inside your brain.
stats.travel: I travel through your nervous system to block pain signals from reaching your brain cells.
stats.wakeup: I wake up when you exercise, laugh out loud, eat spicy food, or when your body feels physical discomfort.

endorphins.job: Endorphins act like your body's built-in shield against pain! When you are tired or hurt from running and playing, I rush in to block the ouch signals and replace them with a happy, energized feeling often called a 'runner's high.'

glitches.low: If there are too little Endorphins, your body might feel more sensitive to minor aches, and you might find it harder to lift your mood when you feel down.
glitches.high: If there are too many Endorphins all the time, your body's natural alarm system gets too quiet, which means you might not notice when you are pushing your muscles too hard or getting injured.

insulin.title: Insulin

stats.travel: I travel through your bloodstream to your muscles, fat, and liver cells.
stats.wakeup: I wake up right after you eat food, especially when your blood sugar starts to rise.

insulin.job: Insulin acts like a master key holder! It unlocks your body's cells so they can let glucose (sugar) inside to be used as energy or stored for later play.

glitches.low: If there is too little Insulin, sugar gets trapped in your blood and your cells starve because they cannot unlock the energy they need.
glitches.high: If there is too much Insulin all the time, your cells get tired of opening their doors and lock their keyholes, causing sugar to pile up outside.

cortisol.title: Cortisol

stats.born: Your adrenal glands, which sit right on top of your kidneys.
stats.travel: I travel everywhere, affecting almost every cell and organ system in your body.
stats.wakeup: I wake up in high amounts early in the morning to help you get out of bed, and whenever you face a sudden challenge.

cortisol.job: Cortisol is your body's built-in alarm system! When you face an urgent situation, it turns up your focus, boosts your energy, and pauses non-essential jobs like digestion so you can handle the challenge completely.

glitches.low: If there is too little Cortisol, your body struggles to wake up properly and cannot handle small physical challenges or sudden changes easily.
glitches.high: If there is too much Cortisol staying around for too long from constant worry, your body remains on high alert, which hurts your sleep and memory.

adrenaline.title: Adrenaline

stats.born: Your adrenal glands, sitting right on top of your kidneys.
stats.travel: I rush through your bloodstream to your heart, lungs, and muscles instantly.
stats.wakeup: I wake up in a flash when you are scared, startled, super excited, or facing danger.

adrenaline.job: Adrenaline is your body's turbo-boost button! When you need to move fast, I make your heart beat faster to pump more oxygen to your muscles, open up your breathing tubes, and give you a sudden burst of super strength and speed to handle emergencies.

glitches.low: If there is too little Adrenaline, your body might react slowly to sudden dangers or sports challenges, making you feel sluggish when you need to move fast.
glitches.high: If there is too much Adrenaline pumping when there is no real danger, you will feel shaky, anxious, and your heart will race for no reason.

melatonin.title: Melatonin

stats.born: The pineal gland, deep inside the center of your brain.
stats.travel: I flood into your brain fluid and bloodstream to reach your entire nervous system.
stats.wakeup: I wake up and start climbing as soon as the sun goes down and your eyes stop seeing bright light.

melatonin.job: Melatonin is your body's night watchman! I don't force you to sleep, but I dim the lights and send a quiet signal across your body letting every organ know that darkness has arrived and it is safely time to rest.

glitches.low: If there is too little Melatonin, your body misses its bedtime cue, making your brain stay wide awake and confused about when to fall asleep.
glitches.high: If your Melatonin levels stay too high during the daytime hours, you will feel sluggish, heavy, and constantly ready to take an accidental nap.

oxytocin.title: Oxytocin

stats.born: Your hypothalamus and released by your pituitary gland in the brain.
stats.travel: I travel through your brain pathways and bloodstream to affect your emotions and heart.
stats.wakeup: I wake up when you hug a family member, pet a dog, share a toy, or hold hands with someone you care about.

oxytocin.job: Oxytocin is your body's friendship glue! When you connect with others, I rush in to lower your stress, make you feel safe, and help you build strong feelings of trust, love, and kindness toward your family and friends.

glitches.low: If there is too little Oxytocin, you might find it harder to connect with others, feel lonely more easily, or struggle to feel comforted when you are upset.
glitches.high: If there is too much Oxytocin, your brain can make you feel overly attached to your closest group, sometimes making you feel nervous or untrustworthy around new people.

dopamine.title: Dopamine

stats.born: Special areas deep inside your brain stem.
stats.travel: I travel along major pathways inside your brain to talk to your behavior centers.
stats.wakeup: I wake up when you celebrate a win, solve a puzzle, find a hidden treat, or look forward to a fun surprise.

dopamine.job: Dopamine is your body's cheerleader! I give you a burst of satisfaction when you do a good job, which trains your brain to remember what success feels like so you stay motivated to practice and try again.

glitches.low: If there is too little Dopamine, you will feel completely drained of energy, find it hard to focus, and lose interest in playing your favorite games.
glitches.high: If there is too much Dopamine floating around, your brain gets stuck chasing quick rewards constantly, making you feel restless and easily distracted.

seratonin.title: Serotonin

stats.born: Your gut cells and special nerve clusters in your brain stem.
stats.travel: I travel through your brain pathways and your entire digestive tract.
stats.wakeup: I wake up when you spend time out in the bright sunshine, eat nutritious food, and get moving.

seratonin.job: Serotonin is your body's mood captain! I help keep your emotions steady, calm, and happy, while also helping your stomach digest your food smoothly and telling your body when it is time to sleep and wake up.

glitches.low: If there is too little Serotonin, you might feel unusually sad, worried, or easily upset, and your tummy might feel grumpy or out of balance.
glitches.high: If there is too much Serotonin, your body's communication wires get mixed up, causing your muscles to twitch and making you feel overly hot or confused.

glucagon.title: Glucagon

stats.travel: I travel through your bloodstream straight to your liver.
stats.wakeup: I wake up when you haven't eaten in a while and your blood sugar drops too low.

glucagon.job: Glucagon is your body's backup energy rescue team! When your blood sugar runs low between meals, I tell your liver to release its stored-up glucose reserves back into your blood, keeping your energy steady until your next snack.

glitches.low: If there is too little Glucagon, your body cannot unlock its stored energy reserves, causing your blood sugar to drop too low and making you feel dizzy, shaky, or weak.
glitches.high: If there is too much Glucagon, your liver constantly dumps extra sugar into your blood, keeping your blood sugar levels too high even when you haven't eaten.

thyroxin.title: Thyroxin

stats.born: Your thyroid gland, which is shaped like a butterfly in your neck.
stats.travel: I travel through your bloodstream to visit almost every single cell in your body.
stats.wakeup: I stay awake constantly, releasing steady amounts to keep your body's systems running smoothly.

thyroxin.job: Thyroxin is your body's speed control dial! I set the pace for your metabolism, deciding how fast your cells turn food into energy, how fast your heart beats, and how quickly your body grows and repairs itself.

glitches.low: If there is too little Thyroxin, your body's engine slows down too much, making you feel cold, extremely tired, and causing your body to grow sluggishly.
glitches.high: If there is too much Thyroxin, your body's engine runs too fast, making your heart race, causing you to sweat, and making you lose weight even when eating plenty of food.

growth.title: Growth Hormone

stats.born: Your pituitary gland, a tiny organ at the base of your brain.
stats.travel: I travel through your bloodstream to find your bones, muscles, and organs.
stats.wakeup: I wake up in big bursts during deep nighttime sleep and right after intense exercise.

growth.job: Growth Hormone is your body's master builder! While you rest, I tell your bones to grow longer, your muscles to get stronger, and your body tissues to repair any tiny scratches or wear-and-tear from a busy day.

glitches.low: If there is too little Growth Hormone, your body will grow much slower than usual, and it will take your muscles longer to heal and recover after running or playing.
glitches.high: If there is too much Growth Hormone, your bones and body parts will grow much faster and larger than normal, making you grow exceptionally tall.

ghrelin.title: Ghrelin

stats.born: Mainly inside your stomach lining when it gets empty.
stats.travel: I travel up through your bloodstream to talk directly to your brain's hunger center.
stats.wakeup: I wake up and get louder whenever your stomach has been empty for a few hours.

ghrelin.job: Ghrelin is your body's dinner bell! I am the hunger monster that makes your stomach rumble. I tell your brain exactly when it is time to stop playing and find a nutritious snack.

glitches.low: If there is too little Ghrelin, your body won't give you clear hunger signals, making you forget to eat and causing your energy levels to drop.
glitches.high: If there is too much Ghrelin, your brain constantly thinks your stomach is empty, making you feel hungry and ready for a meal even right after you just ate.

leptin.title: Leptin

stats.travel: I travel through your bloodstream to the hypothalamus in your brain.
stats.wakeup: I wake up and release steady signals as you eat and store energy from your food.

leptin.job: Leptin is your body's full meter! I talk to your brain to say, 'Hey, we have plenty of fuel in the tank, you can stop eating now!' I help control your appetite so you don't overeat.

glitches.low: If there is too little Leptin, your brain thinks you are starving even when you have eaten plenty, making you feel constantly hungry.
glitches.high: If Leptin stays too high for too long, your brain turns deaf to the signal, ignoring the message that you are full and causing you to keep eating.

estrogen.title: Estrogen

stats.born: Mainly in the ovaries, with small amounts made by adrenal glands.
stats.travel: I travel through the bloodstream to guide bone growth and body changes.
stats.wakeup: I wake up in small amounts during childhood and increase significantly during puberty.

estrogen.job: Estrogen is a master coordinator for growing up! I help keep your bones strong, protect your heart, and guide your body through the natural changes that transform a child into an adult.

glitches.low: If there is too little Estrogen, bones can become brittle and weak, and natural growth milestones during puberty can be delayed.
glitches.high: If there is too much Estrogen, it can disrupt your body's natural timing, causing growth spurts to happen too early or shifting your moods rapidly.

testosterone.title: Testosterone

stats.born: Mainly in the testes, with small amounts made by adrenal glands.
stats.travel: I travel through your bloodstream to work on your muscles, bones, and brain.
stats.wakeup: I release in steady small amounts during childhood and surge during puberty.

testosterone.job: Testosterone is your body's muscle and strength coach! I help build big muscles, make your bones dense and strong, and work behind the scenes to guide growth changes as you get older.

glitches.low: If there is too little Testosterone, your muscles might feel weaker, you may feel tired more often, and natural growth milestones can slow down.
glitches.high: If there is too much Testosterone, it can make your skin break out in spots, cause rapid mood swings, and make you feel unusually restless or grumpy.

running.title: Going for a run
running.summary: During running, your body releases two main hormones.

activeHormones.description: I zoom out from your adrenal glands to instantly widen your lungs and make your heart beat faster. I make sure your muscles get all the oxygen they need to run like the wind!
I run straight to your liver and unlock your emergency sugar tanks, dumping extra fuel into your blood so you dont run out of gas while running!
Normally, I lock sugar away in your bodys closets to save it for later. But right now, I take a break so the sugar can run free into your bloodstream to feed your moving muscles.

stress.title: Spooked or Stressed
stress.summary: When you get a big scare, your body goes on high alert to keep you safe.

activeHormones.description: I am the panic button! I make your heart race, your breathing go shallow, and your eyes wide open so you can spot danger instantly.
I turn off distracting systems like your stomach and immune system. I keep extra sugar running in your blood so your brain stays hyper-focused on the problem!
With a monster around, we cant store energy away! I step aside completely so your body can use every bit of available sugar to deal with the emergency.

fasting.title: Skipping a Meal
fasting.summary: When you havent eaten, your body cleverly switches over to its backup batteries.

activeHormones.description: I am the hunger monster! I rumble your tummy and send loud signals to your brain saying Time to eat! until you get a snack.
Since no new food is coming in, I march straight to your liver and unlock your secret backup fuel tanks to keep your brain powered up.
I am the hormone that tells you when youre full. Since your stomach is completely empty, I disappear so your body knows it needs to hunt for food.

sleeping.title: Deep, Peaceful Sleep
sleeping.summary: While you are having sweet dreams, your body turns into a busy nighttime repair shop.

activeHormones.description: When the lights go out, I flood your brain to signal that it is bedtime. I lower your body temperature and tuck your whole system into a deep, cozy sleep.
While you sleep, I come out to build and repair! I patch up tired muscles, fix up cellular scratches, and help your bones grow a little bit taller every single night.
I am the stress hormone, so I take a deep dive to my lowest level while you sleep. This gives your body a quiet, peaceful break to heal without any daytime distractions.
